summary: Recent updates to the image decoding capabilities in Firefox focus on improving how the browser handles images. One notable change restricts loading to specific types of images, such as page icons and remote images, to enhance security and performance by filtering out unnecessary content. Additionally, enhancements in memory management have been implemented, such as using **refcounted objects**, which help the browser manage image resources more efficiently and prevent memory leaks. Furthermore, support for SVG (Scalable Vector Graphics) has been expanded with new parsing and rendering options, allowing for richer and more flexible image displays on web pages. These changes collectively contribute to a smoother and more secure browsing experience.
